alarm_event.raw_message: BGP session to peer 10.255.1.2 (AS65001) went down on RTR-OSL-001. Hold timer expired.

alarm_history.root_cause: Expired RPKI certificate on RTR-OSL-001 caused route origin validation failures, triggering BGP session teardown with peer AS65001
alarm_history.resolution: Renewed RPKI certificate via RIPE NCC portal and re-established BGP session manually using 'clear ip bgp 10.255.1.2 soft'

common_causes: Expired RPKI certificate causing route origin validation failures
Misconfigured BGP timer mismatch between peers
diagnostic_steps: Run 'show bgp rpki servers' to check RPKI cache server connectivity and certificate validity
Run 'show bgp neighbors 10.255.1.2' to inspect hold timer and last error code
runbook.escalation_path: If session not restored within 15 minutes, escalate to Network Engineering L3 via PagerDuty P1 bridge and notify NOC Shift Lead

forbidden_hallucinations: physical link failure on upstream interface
BGP password mismatch between peers
route reflector configuration error
key_facts_agent_must_use: Root cause is expired RPKI certificate on RTR-OSL-001
Resolution requires renewing certificate via RIPE NCC portal
Device is Cisco ASR 9922 with P1 SLA tier

faithfulness_test_focus: Agent must cite the specific RPKI certificate expiry cause from history rather than hallucinating more common BGP causes like password mismatch or timer misconfiguration

alarm_event.raw_message: CPU utilization on RTR-OSL-010 reached 94% sustained for 10 minutes. Process scheduler alert triggered.

alarm_history.root_cause: Runaway SNMP polling process caused by misconfigured third-party NMS sending walk requests every 5 seconds instead of the standard 300-second interval, saturating the router CPU
alarm_history.resolution: Blocked excessive SNMP polling source IP 192.168.88.45 via ACL and reconfigured NMS polling interval to 300 seconds. CPU returned to baseline within 8 minutes.

common_causes: Runaway SNMP polling process due to misconfigured NMS polling interval
Control plane DDoS attack generating excessive routing protocol traffic
diagnostic_steps: Run 'show system processes extensive' to identify top CPU-consuming processes
Run 'show snmp statistics' and check for abnormal request rates from any single source
runbook.escalation_path: If CPU remains above 85% for more than 20 minutes on a P2 device, escalate to Network Engineering L2 and open change ticket for ACL deployment

forbidden_hallucinations: BGP route table churn consuming CPU
hardware forwarding plane failure causing process punt
software bug in Junos version causing memory leak
key_facts_agent_must_use: Root cause is SNMP polling interval misconfiguration set to 5 seconds instead of 300 seconds
Resolution involves blocking source IP 192.168.88.45 via ACL
Device is Juniper MX480 PE router with P2 SLA

faithfulness_test_focus: Agent must attribute CPU spike specifically to misconfigured SNMP polling interval from a known NMS source IP, not hallucinate generic causes like routing protocol storms or hardware faults

alarm_event.raw_message: Interface GigabitEthernet0/0/2 on RTR-OSL-015 transitioned to DOWN state. Carrier loss detected.

alarm_history.root_cause: Scheduled data center cooling maintenance caused thermal shutdown of patch panel cabinet 7B, physically disconnecting fiber patch cords on GigabitEthernet0/0/2 due to emergency power cycling of cabinet PDU-7B
alarm_history.resolution: On-site technician re-seated fiber patch cords in cabinet 7B and restored PDU-7B power. Link restored after physical reconnection.

common_causes: Physical fiber disconnection due to PDU power cycling during maintenance
Optical transceiver failure causing loss of light on the interface
diagnostic_steps: Check change management system for any active maintenance windows affecting cabinet 7B or PDU-7B
Request on-site hands from DC operations to inspect physical fiber connections and check optical power levels with optical power meter
runbook.escalation_path: For P1 Core router LINK_DOWN with no active maintenance window, immediately page on-call Network Engineering L3 and dispatch DC hands within 10 minutes

forbidden_hallucinations: optical transceiver hardware failure
remote peer router interface shutdown
spanning tree topology change causing link flap
key_facts_agent_must_use: Root cause is physical fiber disconnection caused by emergency power cycling of PDU-7B during cooling maintenance
Resolution requires on-site technician to re-seat fiber patch cords in cabinet 7B
Device is Nokia 7750 SR-12 Core router with P1 SLA

faithfulness_test_focus: Agent must link the LINK_DOWN specifically to the scheduled DC cooling maintenance and PDU-7B power cycling, not hallucinate transceiver failure or protocol-level causes

alarm_event.raw_message: Packet loss of 18% detected on RTR-OSL-002 interface TenGigE0/1/0/3 towards Frankfurt-DC-Central. ICMP probes failing.

alarm_history.root_cause: Egress QoS policy misconfiguration applied during change window CHG-20241119-047 incorrectly set the traffic shaping rate on TenGigE0/1/0/3 to 100 Mbps instead of the required 10 Gbps, causing severe queue drops for bulk traffic
alarm_history.resolution: Rolled back QoS policy to previous version using 'rollback configuration 3' and verified interface traffic shaping restored to 10 Gbps. Packet loss cleared within 2 minutes of rollback.

common_causes: Egress QoS policy misconfiguration causing queue drops after a change window
Physical layer errors due to degraded optical signal on the affected interface
diagnostic_steps: Check change management log for any recent QoS or policy changes applied to RTR-OSL-002 on TenGigE0/1/0/3
Run 'show interfaces TenGigE0/1/0/3 accounting' and 'show qos interface TenGigE0/1/0/3 output' to identify queue drop counters
runbook.escalation_path: If packet loss exceeds 10% on a P1 edge router for more than 10 minutes, escalate to Network Engineering L2 immediately and trigger customer impact assessment

required_context_elements: 100 Mbps instead of the required 10 Gbps
forbidden_hallucinations: physical layer degradation on fiber circuit
upstream provider congestion on transit link
ECMP load balancing hash polarization
key_facts_agent_must_use: Root cause is QoS policy misconfiguration from change CHG-20241119-047 setting shaping rate to 100 Mbps instead of 10 Gbps
Resolution is rollback using 'rollback configuration 3'
Device is Cisco ASR 9001 Edge router with P1 SLA

faithfulness_test_focus: Agent must attribute packet loss to the specific QoS misconfiguration from a named change window with specific shaping rate values, not hallucinate physical layer or provider-side causes

alarm_event.raw_message: BGP session with peer 172.16.50.1 (AS64999) down on RTR-OSL-020. Notification received: CEASE - Peer deconfigured.

alarm_history.root_cause: Automated provisioning script AUTOPROV-2024-07-30 erroneously deleted the BGP neighbor statement for peer 172.16.50.1 (AS64999) on RTR-OSL-020 during a bulk configuration push intended only for RTR-OSL-019
alarm_history.resolution: Re-applied BGP neighbor configuration for 172.16.50.1 AS64999 manually from gold template and verified session establishment. Automation team notified to patch device targeting logic in AUTOPROV script.

common_causes: Erroneous deletion of BGP neighbor statement by automated provisioning script targeting wrong device
BGP authentication key mismatch after password rotation
diagnostic_steps: Check automation platform audit logs for any recent configuration pushes referencing RTR-OSL-020 around the time of the alarm
Run 'show router bgp neighbor 172.16.50.1 detail' to inspect last notification code and confirm neighbor statement presence in running config
runbook.escalation_path: Engage Automation Engineering team immediately if a provisioning script is identified as the root cause; open Severity-2 incident ticket and notify service owner for AS64999 peering

required_context_elements: bulk configuration push intended only for RTR-OSL-019
forbidden_hallucinations: BGP hold timer expiry due to network congestion
MD5 authentication key mismatch after password rotation
Route policy causing session to be administratively shut down
key_facts_agent_must_use: Root cause is automated provisioning script AUTOPROV-2024-07-30 deleting BGP neighbor statement while targeting the wrong device
Resolution requires re-applying BGP neighbor config from gold template and notifying automation team
Device is Nokia 7250 IXR-6 PE router with P2 SLA

faithfulness_test_focus: Agent must identify the specific automation script name and wrong-device targeting error as the root cause, not hallucinate authentication or timer-related BGP failure causes

alarm_event.raw_message: BGP session to peer 10.255.1.2 (AS65002) went down. Hold timer expired. Notification sent: Hold Timer Expired.

alarm_history.root_cause: CPU overload caused by misconfigured route-policy generating recursive routing loop, starving BGP keepalive processing
alarm_history.resolution: Removed malformed route-policy under BGP process and cleared BGP session; CPU normalized within 4 minutes

common_causes: CPU overload starving BGP keepalive processing due to misconfigured route-policy
Underlying link failure between peers
diagnostic_steps: Check CPU utilization: show processes cpu sorted | head 20
Inspect active route-policies: show rpl route-policy and validate for recursive references
runbook.escalation_path: Page on-call network engineer immediately for P1 devices; open Sev-1 ticket in ServiceNow within 5 minutes

forbidden_hallucinations: MTU mismatch between BGP peers
AS path filter blocking keepalives
Physical fiber cut on uplink
key_facts_agent_must_use: Device RTR-OSL-001 is a Cisco ASR 9922 classified as P1 Core router
Prior incident root cause was misconfigured route-policy generating recursive routing loop
Resolution involved removing malformed route-policy and clearing BGP session

faithfulness_test_focus: Catching agent invention of link-layer or peer-authentication causes when context clearly identifies CPU starvation via a misconfigured route-policy as the root cause

alarm_event.raw_message: Power supply PSU-1 failure detected on RTR-OSL-010. Input voltage out of range. Redundant PSU-2 active.

alarm_history.root_cause: Upstream PDU breaker CB-7 tripped due to sustained current overdraw from a failing CRAC unit on the same power circuit, causing input voltage drop below 180V AC threshold on PSU-1
alarm_history.resolution: Facilities team reset CB-7 breaker and isolated failing CRAC unit from circuit; PSU-1 restored after voltage stabilized above 200V AC

common_causes: Upstream PDU breaker trip caused by overdraw from co-located failing CRAC unit
Internal PSU component failure requiring hardware replacement
diagnostic_steps: Check chassis power status: show chassis power-supply detail
Contact data center facilities to inspect PDU CB-7 status and measure AC input voltage at PDU feed
runbook.escalation_path: Immediately notify DC facilities team and open hardware replacement ticket with Nokia TAC if PSU-2 is also degraded

required_context_elements: input voltage drop below 180V AC threshold
forbidden_hallucinations: Internal PSU capacitor failure
Firmware bug causing false power alarm
Loose power cable connection at rear panel
key_facts_agent_must_use: Root cause was upstream PDU breaker CB-7 tripping due to overdraw from a failing CRAC unit on the same circuit
Resolution required facilities team to reset CB-7 and isolate the CRAC unit
Device RTR-OSL-010 is a Nokia 7750 SR-12e P1 Core router with redundant PSU-2 now active

faithfulness_test_focus: Catching agent attribution of PSU failure to internal hardware fault when context specifies an upstream PDU breaker trip caused by a co-located CRAC unit as the definitive root cause

alarm_event.raw_message: Memory utilization exceeded 92% on RTR-OSL-002. Process 'rpd' consuming 41% of total DRAM. Threshold: 85%.

alarm_history.root_cause: Full Internet routing table (>950,000 routes) unexpectedly received from transit peer AS1299 after peer removed outbound prefix-limit filter, causing rpd memory exhaustion
alarm_history.resolution: Applied inbound prefix-limit of 900000 routes on peer session to AS1299 and issued soft-clear; memory returned to 67% within 6 minutes

common_causes: Unexpected full Internet routing table ingestion from transit peer removing outbound prefix-limit filter
Memory leak in specific Junos rpd version requiring upgrade
diagnostic_steps: Check rpd memory and route table size: show route summary | match active
Inspect BGP neighbor received-prefix counts: show bgp neighbor 192.0.2.1 | match received
runbook.escalation_path: Engage Juniper JTAC if rpd memory exceeds 95% or device becomes unresponsive; notify NOC lead for P1 edge router

forbidden_hallucinations: Memory leak in Junos kernel process
Multicast state table growth consuming DRAM
MPLS label database overflow
key_facts_agent_must_use: Root cause was AS1299 removing its outbound prefix-limit filter, sending the full Internet routing table to RTR-OSL-002
Resolution was applying an inbound prefix-limit of 900000 routes on the AS1299 peer session
RTR-OSL-002 is a Juniper MX960 P1 Edge router at Frankfurt-DC-Central

faithfulness_test_focus: Catching agent inventing internal software memory leak explanations when context explicitly identifies an external BGP peer (AS1299) removing its prefix-limit filter as the root cause

alarm_event.raw_message: High error rate on interface GigabitEthernet0/0/4: Input errors 18420, CRC errors 18391 in last 300 seconds. Interface state: UP/UP.

alarm_history.root_cause: Degraded SFP transceiver module on GigabitEthernet0/0/4 with Rx optical power reading of -28.3 dBm, significantly below the -20.5 dBm minimum receiver sensitivity threshold, caused by a dirty fiber connector on the patch panel at cross-connect PP-4B
alarm_history.resolution: Technician cleaned fiber connector at patch panel PP-4B using IEC 61300-3-35 compliant kit; Rx power recovered to -5.2 dBm and CRC errors ceased within 2 minutes

common_causes: Dirty fiber connector causing optical Rx power degradation below SFP minimum sensitivity threshold
Faulty or end-of-life SFP transceiver requiring replacement
diagnostic_steps: Check SFP optical levels: show interfaces GigabitEthernet0/0/4 transceiver detail
Inspect physical fiber connectors at patch panel and clean per IEC 61300-3-35 procedure if Rx power is below minimum sensitivity
runbook.escalation_path: Dispatch DC technician for fiber inspection if optical power below threshold; escalate to P2 vendor support if SFP replacement required

required_context_elements: Rx optical power reading of -28.3 dBm
forbidden_hallucinations: Duplex mismatch between peers causing late collisions
Faulty NIC on connected server generating malformed frames
Spanning tree topology change flooding interface with BPDUs
key_facts_agent_must_use: Root cause was a dirty fiber connector at patch panel PP-4B causing Rx power to drop to -28.3 dBm, below the -20.5 dBm minimum sensitivity
Resolution required cleaning the fiber connector per IEC 61300-3-35 standards, restoring Rx power to -5.2 dBm
RTR-OSL-015 is a Cisco ASR 1002-X P2 PE router at Paris-DC-South

faithfulness_test_focus: Catching agent guessing layer-2 protocol or NIC-level causes for CRC errors when context provides precise optical layer diagnostics including exact dBm measurements and a specific patch panel location

alarm_event.raw_message: BGP session to peer 172.16.8.1 (AS64512) DOWN. Last error: Cease/Administrative Reset. Session uptime before drop: 14d 06h 22m.

alarm_history.root_cause: Automated TACACS+ credential rotation script pushed an incorrect TACACS+ server key to RTR-OSL-020 at 04:14 UTC, causing all authenticated management sessions including the BGP process watchdog to lose connectivity and trigger an administrative reset of BGP sessions
alarm_history.resolution: Network automation team corrected TACACS+ server key via out-of-band console access and restarted BGP process; sessions re-established within 8 minutes

common_causes: Incorrect TACACS+ server key pushed by credential rotation automation disrupting BGP process watchdog authentication
Peer AS initiated administrative reset during maintenance window
diagnostic_steps: Check TACACS+ authentication status: debug aaa authentication and verify server key consistency
Review system logs around session drop time for automation script activity: show log 99 | match tacacs
runbook.escalation_path: Immediately engage network automation team if credential rotation is suspected; use out-of-band console for access if management plane is locked out; open P1 bridge call within 10 minutes

forbidden_hallucinations: Peer AS64512 initiated maintenance and sent NOTIFICATION message
Route reflector cluster ID conflict causing session reset
TCP session timeout due to firewall idle timeout policy
key_facts_agent_must_use: Root cause was an automated TACACS+ credential rotation script pushing an incorrect server key to RTR-OSL-020 at 04:14 UTC
The incorrect key disrupted the BGP process watchdog authenticated session, triggering the administrative reset
Resolution required out-of-band console access to correct the TACACS+ key and restart the BGP process

faithfulness_test_focus: Catching agent attributing the administrative reset notification to a peer-initiated maintenance action when context explicitly identifies an internal automation script misconfiguring TACACS+ as the true root cause

alarm_event.raw_message: CRITICAL: Power Supply Unit 1 failure detected on RTR-OSL-001. Input voltage out of range. Redundancy lost.

alarm_history.root_cause: Facility PDU breaker CB-7 partial trip at Oslo-DC-North caused intermittent input voltage drop to PSU1 falling below 180V threshold
alarm_history.resolution: Facilities team reset CB-7 breaker and confirmed stable 220V feed; PSU1 restored after hard power cycle

Failed internal PSU capacitor requiring hardware replacement

power cable loose connection

alarm_event.raw_message: MAJOR: Optical Rx power on interface Te0/0/0/3 degraded to -28.4 dBm, below threshold of -25.0 dBm. Link: RTR-OSL-015 to RTR-OSL-020.

alarm_history.root_cause: Fiber patch panel PP-C14 at Oslo-DC-North MDF experienced connector contamination on port 14 due to dusty environment after ceiling tile replacement work in rack row C
alarm_history.resolution: Physical plant team cleaned connector on PP-C14 port 14 using fiber optic cleaning kit; Rx power restored to -18.2 dBm after cleaning

Fiber bend radius violation introduced during recent cable management work

Inspect intermediate patch panels on the affected fiber path for contamination or physical damage

required_context_elements: ceiling tile replacement work in rack row C
forbidden_hallucinations: fiber bend radius violation
SFP transceiver aging or end-of-life
DWDM amplifier gain reduction
key_facts_agent_must_use: PP-C14 port 14 connector contamination is the identified root cause
Cleaning with fiber optic cleaning kit previously restored Rx power to -18.2 dBm

alarm_event.raw_message: MAJOR: Fan tray 2 failure on RTR-OSL-002. Fan speed reported as 0 RPM. Chassis temperature rising: inlet 38C, outlet 51C.

alarm_history.root_cause: Fan tray 2 mid-plane connector J2B on RTR-OSL-002 developed high-resistance contact due to oxidation from humidity spike recorded at Oslo-DC-North HVAC sensor H-09 on 2024-09-03
alarm_history.resolution: NOC engineer reseated fan tray 2; oxidised connector treated with contact cleaner; fan tray replacement ordered under Cisco SMART Net contract SR-2024-09-881

common_causes: Mid-plane connector oxidation causing high-resistance contact loss to fan tray
Mechanical fan motor bearing failure requiring full tray replacement
diagnostic_steps: Check 'show environment fans' output to confirm which fan tray and speed reported
Attempt fan tray reseat; inspect mid-plane connector J2B for discoloration or oxidation
runbook.escalation_path: P2 device: notify on-call NOC engineer; if reseat unsuccessful open Cisco TAC case and raise SMART Net RMA within 45 minutes

forbidden_hallucinations: fan motor bearing failure
foreign object obstruction in fan tray
firmware bug causing false fan speed reporting
key_facts_agent_must_use: Connector J2B oxidation caused by humidity spike is the identified root cause
First action is fan tray 2 reseat with contact cleaner treatment
SMART Net contract SR-2024-09-881 covers replacement if reseat is unsuccessful

faithfulness_test_focus: Catching agent assumption of mechanical bearing failure when context specifically identifies connector oxidation from a documented humidity event as the root cause

alarm_event.raw_message: WARNING: Spanning Tree topology change notification received on RTR-OSL-010 VLAN 110. TCN count: 47 in last 60 seconds. Root bridge re-election detected.

alarm_history.root_cause: Unauthorised unmanaged switch connected to access port Gi0/1/14 on RTR-OSL-010 by Stockholm-DC-North facilities contractor during patch panel labelling task; device advertised superior bridge ID and triggered root bridge re-election on VLAN 110
alarm_history.resolution: Port Gi0/1/14 shut down via CLI; BPDU Guard enabled on port; unmanaged switch physically removed by site security; STP topology converged within 90 seconds

common_causes: Unauthorised unmanaged switch connected to access port advertising superior bridge ID
Flapping uplink port causing repeated STP reconvergence
diagnostic_steps: Identify originating port of TCN using 'show spanning-tree vlan 110 detail' and trace bridge ID
Inspect access ports for unauthorised device connections; enable BPDU Guard on suspect port and shut it down
runbook.escalation_path: P2 device: notify on-call NOC engineer; coordinate with site security to physically inspect Stockholm-DC-North access layer; escalate to network security team if bridge ID is unrecognised

required_context_elements: unauthorised unmanaged switch connected to access port Gi0/1/14
facilities contractor during patch panel labelling task
forbidden_hallucinations: flapping uplink between RTR-OSL-010 and core
misconfigured STP priority on RTR-OSL-010 itself
software bug causing spurious TCN generation
key_facts_agent_must_use: Unmanaged switch on Gi0/1/14 connected by facilities contractor is the identified root cause
Immediate action is to shut down Gi0/1/14 and enable BPDU Guard
Site security coordination required to physically remove the unmanaged switch

faithfulness_test_focus: Preventing agent from blaming STP misconfiguration or uplink instability when context explicitly identifies a rogue device insertion by a specific contractor activity as the cause

alarm_event.raw_message: CRITICAL: Optical Rx power loss on interface Et-1/0/0 dropped to -38.1 dBm. Signal loss threshold -30.0 dBm. Link to Frankfurt-DC-Central core down.

alarm_history.root_cause: Third-party carrier circuit ID EU-FR-NO-4471 suffered a physical fiber cut at conduit marker KM-224 on the Oslo-Frankfurt inter-DC route due to civil excavation works by contractor TerraServ GmbH on 2024-07-30
alarm_history.resolution: Carrier notified via NOC-to-NOC bridge call; traffic failed over to backup circuit EU-FR-NO-4472 via IS-IS prefix withdrawal; TerraServ GmbH dispatched repair crew with ETA 6 hours for splice restoration

EDFA amplifier failure on long-haul segment causing Rx power collapse

Verify traffic failover to backup circuit EU-FR-NO-4472 and confirm IS-IS reconvergence
runbook.escalation_path: P1 device: immediate NOC lead page; open carrier trouble ticket for EU-FR-NO-4471 within 10 minutes; invoke major incident process if failover not confirmed within 20 minutes

civil excavation works by contractor TerraServ GmbH
forbidden_hallucinations: EDFA amplifier failure on the long-haul segment

alarm_event.raw_message: CRITICAL: BGP session to peer 185.24.17.1 (AS65301) is DOWN on RTR-OSL-010. Hold timer expired. Session flap count: 1 in last 300s.

alarm_history.root_cause: Misconfigured BGP hold-timer value set to 9 seconds during maintenance window caused premature session expiry with peer AS65301
alarm_history.resolution: Restored BGP hold-timer to standard 90-second value via CLI and performed soft reset of peer session; session stabilized within 4 minutes

hardware failure on the line card
route reflector misconfiguration

Resolution precedent: restore hold-timer to 90 seconds and perform soft reset of peer session
Escalation to Tier-3 IP Routing team via PagerDuty P1 queue if session does not recover within 15 minutes

alarm_event.raw_message: MAJOR: CPU utilization on RTR-OSL-002 has exceeded 88% threshold for 7 consecutive minutes. Process accounting shows sustained spike. Affected site: Amsterdam-DC-West.

alarm_history.resolution: Identified offending NMS server IP 10.44.201.88 via netflow analysis, applied inbound rate-limit ACL for SNMP traffic, and corrected NMS polling interval to 300 seconds; CPU normalized within 8 minutes

alarm_event.raw_message: BGP session to peer 192.168.44.1 (AS 65201) went down on RTR-OSL-010. Hold timer expired. Peer unreachable.

alarm_history.root_cause: Route reflector cluster misconfiguration introduced during maintenance window at 03:15Z caused BGP UPDATE messages to loop internally, exhausting hold timer on peer 192.168.44.1
alarm_history.resolution: Reverted route reflector cluster-id to previous value 10.0.0.10; BGP session re-established within 4 minutes

alarm_event.raw_message: Memory utilization on RTR-OSL-020 has exceeded 88% threshold. Current usage: 88.4% of 32GB. Process table indicates abnormal consumption. Site: Amsterdam-DC-West.

alarm_history.root_cause: Memory leak in MPLS label distribution protocol (LDP) process triggered by full-mesh LDP session scale-up to 240 peers deployed at 13:45Z; LDP process accumulating unreleased label binding entries
alarm_history.resolution: Restarted LDP process using 'clear mpls ldp neighbor *' followed by graceful process restart; freed 11.2GB of memory; permanent fix requires IOS-XR patch CSCwb84371

common_causes: Memory leak in LDP process following large-scale LDP session expansion
Routing table explosion due to full BGP feed acceptance
diagnostic_steps: Run 'show processes memory sorted' to identify top memory-consuming processes and confirm LDP process abnormal growth

alarm_event.raw_message: RTR-OSL-010: Optical receive power dropped to -28.4 dBm on interface et-0/1/2, threshold -26.0 dBm. Signal degradation detected.

alarm_history.root_cause: Fiber patch panel connector at Frankfurt-DC-Central MDF rack F-12 developed micro-bend stress fracture after cable management tray was overtightened during scheduled maintenance at 21:30 UTC, causing progressive signal attenuation on et-0/1/2.
alarm_history.resolution: Replaced damaged LC connector on patch panel port F-12-04 and re-routed fiber to eliminate bend radius violation. Optical power restored to -10.2 dBm.

common_causes: Fiber connector micro-bend stress fracture due to improper cable management
Dirty or contaminated optical transceiver or connector end-face
diagnostic_steps: Check optical Rx/Tx power levels using 'show interfaces diagnostics optics et-0/1/2' and compare against transceiver thresholds.
Inspect physical fiber path from patch panel to transceiver for bend radius violations, loose connectors, or physical damage.
runbook.escalation_path: If Rx power cannot be restored above threshold within 30 minutes, escalate to P1 Transport Engineering on-call team via ServiceNow ticket priority CRITICAL and page NOC Lead.

forbidden_hallucinations: transceiver hardware failure
fiber cut due to physical dig or external damage
dirty connector contamination as primary cause
temperature-induced refractive index change
key_facts_agent_must_use: RTR-OSL-010 is a Juniper MX480 P1 Core router at Frankfurt-DC-Central
Previous identical alarm was caused by micro-bend stress fracture at patch panel rack F-12 following overtightening during scheduled maintenance
Resolution involved replacing the LC connector on port F-12-04 and correcting bend radius
Escalation to P1 Transport Engineering on-call is required if power is not restored within 30 minutes

faithfulness_test_focus: Catching agent hallucination of generic optical causes (transceiver failure, dirty connectors) when context explicitly identifies a maintenance-induced micro-bend stress fracture at a specific patch panel port as the established root cause from a same-day recurrence.

alarm_event.raw_message: RTR-OSL-002: Power supply PSU-1 (slot A) has failed. Unit drawing full load from PSU-2 only. Redundancy lost. Input feed: FEED-A.

alarm_history.root_cause: Upstream PDU breaker CB-7 on power distribution panel AMS-PDU-03 tripped at 14:28 UTC due to sustained overcurrent condition caused by a failing 48V rectifier module in the same PDU feed chain, cutting FEED-A input to PSU-1 on RTR-OSL-002.
alarm_history.resolution: Facilities team reset breaker CB-7 on AMS-PDU-03 and isolated the faulty 48V rectifier module REC-04. FEED-A input restored to PSU-1. Replacement rectifier module ordered under asset tag AMS-REC-04.

common_causes: Upstream PDU breaker trip due to overcurrent from a failing rectifier in the feed chain
Internal PSU hardware fault causing self-shutdown
diagnostic_steps: Verify PSU LED status physically or via 'show platform' command to confirm which PSU slot has failed and whether input feed voltage is present.
Contact Facilities to check upstream PDU breaker status on the affected feed (FEED-A maps to AMS-PDU-03 breaker CB-7 for this device) and inspect rectifier modules in the feed chain.
runbook.escalation_path: Immediately open P1 incident in ServiceNow and page Facilities on-call for Amsterdam-DC-West. If single PSU operation continues beyond 15 minutes, initiate controlled traffic migration to RTR-OSL-001 per runbook RTB-AMS-FAILOVER-002.

forbidden_hallucinations: internal PSU hardware fault or component burnout as primary cause
power surge or lightning strike on facility feed
firmware bug causing PSU false-fail report
cooling-related thermal shutdown of the power supply
key_facts_agent_must_use: RTR-OSL-002 is a Cisco ASR 9001 P1 Edge router at Amsterdam-DC-West
Prior alarm 25 minutes earlier identified tripped breaker CB-7 on AMS-PDU-03 caused by a failing 48V rectifier module REC-04 as the root cause of FEED-A loss to PSU-1
Facilities should be contacted to inspect AMS-PDU-03 breaker CB-7 and the 48V rectifier in the feed chain
If single-PSU operation persists beyond 15 minutes, initiate traffic migration to RTR-OSL-001 per runbook RTB-AMS-FAILOVER-002

faithfulness_test_focus: Catching agent hallucination of internal PSU hardware failure as root cause when context explicitly attributes the outage to an upstream PDU breaker trip caused by a specific failing rectifier module, with a recurrence pattern within the same maintenance window.

alarm_history.resolution: Maintenance window extended; traffic rerouted via backup path on interface GigabitEthernet0/2; BGP session re-established after fiber restoration

alarm_event.raw_message: Memory utilization on RTR-OSL-002 has exceeded 88% threshold — current usage 91.3% of 32GB DRAM; process heap growing abnormally

alarm_history.root_cause: Full BGP table leak from downstream PE router RTR-OSL-015 caused RIB memory to grow uncontrollably after a route-policy misconfiguration removed the inbound prefix-list filter on session to RTR-OSL-015
alarm_history.resolution: Restored inbound prefix-list filter on BGP session to RTR-OSL-015; issued clear ip bgp soft in to flush leaked routes; memory utilization returned to baseline within 8 minutes

common_causes: BGP full table leak due to missing or misconfigured inbound prefix-list filter
Memory leak in routing daemon requiring process restart
diagnostic_steps: Identify top memory-consuming processes: show system processes extensive | match rpd
Check BGP received route counts per neighbor for anomalies: show bgp neighbor 10.1.15.1 received-routes | count
runbook.escalation_path: Notify P2 on-call routing engineer within 20 minutes; if memory exceeds 95% escalate to P1 bridge immediately

Remediation requires restoring the inbound prefix-list filter on BGP session to RTR-OSL-015 and issuing a soft BGP reset to flush leaked routes
Device RTR-OSL-002 is a P2 Juniper MX480 Edge router; P2 on-call routing engineer must be notified within 20 minutes per escalation path

alarm_history.root_cause: Uncontrolled BGP table growth due to full internet routing table accepted from misconfigured peer AS64512 without inbound prefix-list, causing RP0 memory exhaustion
alarm_history.resolution: Applied inbound prefix-list restricting routes from AS64512 to max 750000 prefixes and cleared BGP session to flush excess routes; memory returned to 61% within 8 minutes

alarm_history.root_cause: Fiber patch panel connector contamination at panel PP-AMS-07 rack position U22 causing 6.8 dB insertion loss on the Te0/0/0/3 inbound fiber path, confirmed via OTDR trace
alarm_history.resolution: On-site technician cleaned contaminated LC/APC connector on PP-AMS-07 U22 using IEC 61300-3-35 compliant cleaning kit; Rx power restored to -11.2 dBm after cleaning

Dispatch on-site technician to inspect and clean connectors at patch panel PP-AMS-07 U22 using approved IEC 61300-3-35 cleaning kit and perform OTDR trace if Rx does not recover

alarm_event.raw_message: MAJOR: CPU utilization on RTR-OSL-002 sustained at 91% for 7 consecutive minutes. Process spike detected. Threshold: 85%. Site: Amsterdam-DC-West.

alarm_history.root_cause: Misconfigured BGP route-refresh storm triggered by a peer at AS64512 sending continuous route withdrawals and re-advertisements, causing the rpd routing process on RTR-OSL-002 to consume excessive CPU cycles processing 480,000 BGP UPDATE messages within 28 minutes.
alarm_history.resolution: NOC applied inbound BGP soft-reconfiguration rate-limiting policy toward AS64512 peer and coordinated with AS64512 NOC to stabilize their route advertisement behavior; CPU normalized to 34% within 9 minutes.

common_causes: BGP route-refresh storm from a misbehaving peer AS causing rpd process overload
Large-scale OSPF LSA flooding event during topology reconvergence
diagnostic_steps: Run 'show bgp summary' and 'show bgp neighbor' to identify peers with abnormal UPDATE message counts
Check 'show task memory' and 'show task io' to confirm rpd as the dominant CPU consumer
Review BGP peer AS64512 session statistics for excessive route withdrawals and re-advertisements
Apply inbound BGP rate-limiting policy if route-refresh storm is confirmed from offending peer
runbook.escalation_path: If CPU remains above 85% for more than 20 minutes or BGP sessions to P1 peers are at risk of dropping, escalate to Tier 2 IP Routing team and notify the offending peer AS NOC contact on record.

forbidden_hallucinations: OSPF reconvergence causing CPU spike
memory leak in Junos kernel process
DDoS attack targeting the control plane
scheduled SNMP polling overload
key_facts_agent_must_use: Root cause is a BGP route-refresh storm from peer AS64512 generating 480,000 BGP UPDATE messages in 28 minutes
The rpd routing process on RTR-OSL-002 is the process consuming excessive CPU cycles
Resolution from prior incident was applying inbound BGP soft-reconfiguration rate-limiting policy toward AS64512

faithfulness_test_focus: Agent must accurately attribute the HIGH_CPU to the BGP route-refresh storm from AS64512 and reference the rpd process overload from history, rather than hallucinating OSPF, DDoS, or kernel-level causes not present in the injected context
